response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced software craftsman with 20+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally unqualified for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — the job lists native-level Japanese as a hard requirement, yet the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language barrier, his technical background is centered on web development, TypeScript/Node.js, and SaaS products, with no demonstrable experience in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), L2/L3 networking, embedded firmware, Linux kernel modification, or LAN-switch development. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2007–2008, which is too dated and shallow for this role's requirements.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software development experience across embedded, web, and systems domains
Demonstrated low-latency and systems-level thinking from work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Early embedded and hardware-adjacent experience (robotic arms, wave field synthesis, NI LabWindows C/C++ components)
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP, switch SDKs) or embedded networking firmware
C programming exposure is minimal and nearly two decades out of date; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web

key_factors.factor: No network security or embedded networking experience
